## Root Cause
The error is caused by the inability to install libraries on serverless compute due to a mismatch in the Python version or platform in the wheel files.

## Explanation
The Databricks notebook is trying to install libraries from a wheelhouse directory, but the installation is failing because the wheel files are not compatible with the Python version or platform being used by the serverless compute.

## Fix Description
The fix involves ensuring that the wheel files in the wheelhouse directory are compatible with the Python version and platform used by the serverless compute. This can be achieved by regenerating the wheelhouse directory using the same Python version as the Databricks runtime.

## Corrected Code
```python
To fix this issue, you need to regenerate the wheelhouse directory. You can do this by running the following commands in your local environment with the same Python version as your Databricks cluster:

```
# Assuming you are using Python 3.12
python -m pip wheel -r requirements.txt -w wheelhouse --no-cache-dir
```
Then, update your Databricks notebook to use the newly generated wheelhouse directory.
```
